title: Merck — RSV — Synthesis
html:
Synthesis: Enflonsia vs. Beyfortus Competitive Battle (as of April 2026)

Governing Thought

After its first full RSV season on market (Oct 2025 - Mar 2026), Enflonsia has established operational differentiation but failed to close the reach and credibility gap with Beyfortus. The data paints a clear picture: HCPs who hear the Enflonsia story like it (high message effectiveness), but too few are hearing it (50% PP reach vs. 80% Beyfortus), and those who hear it still won't switch mid-season. The 2026-27 pre-season window (July-September 2026) is Enflonsia's true first test -- everything so far has been warm-up.

Five Things That Matter Most Right Now

1. Enflonsia's Identity Is Established: It's the "Simple Dose" Product

The same-dose-regardless-of-weight message (ENF2) dominates every metric: highest unaided recall, highest aided recall (84%), highest message effectiveness (79%), strongest brand association, and highest correlation with stocking intent. This is not a positioning question anymore -- it's settled. The risk is over-rotation: Enflonsia must complement dosing with efficacy and access messaging, not replace it.

ZoomRx implication: PET tracking should monitor whether ENF2 recall begins to plateau (ceiling effect) and whether secondary messages (ENF3/efficacy, ENF14/access) gain traction.

2. The Reach Gap Is the Binding Constraint

Beyfortus reaches 80% of target MDs via personal promotion vs. 50% for Enflonsia. On NPP, it's 95% vs. 41%. This isn't a messaging problem -- it's a sales force deployment and NPP investment problem. Enflonsia can have the best messages in the industry and still lose if half the market never sees a rep.

ZoomRx implication: Track reach trends Q-over-Q. If Enflonsia PP reach doesn't hit 65%+ by Q3 '26 (pre-season), the 2026-27 season is likely a repeat of 2025-26.

3. The Real-World Evidence Gap Is Beyfortus's Trump Card

Beyfortus's "6M+ infants immunized" (BEYF12) is among its most effective messages (67% ME). Enflonsia has nothing to counter. HCPs increasingly want to see RWE before committing. This gap widens every month Enflonsia is on market without publishing real-world data.

ZoomRx implication: Monitor when Merck introduces any RWE messaging. The moment they do, it changes the competitive dynamic fundamentally.

4. Stocking Barriers Are Shifting Favorably -- But Slowly

The top barrier ("committed to Beyfortus") declined 26 points Q4->Q1, signaling that some practices are open to considering alternatives for next season. But new barriers emerged: "practice policy: only stock one product" (+10%) and "financial uncertainty" (+8%). The net position is improving but not fast enough.

ZoomRx implication: The Oct '26-Mar '27 stocking likelihood question added in Q1 '26 is the single most important forward-looking metric in the PET study.

5. The External Environment Is Complicated

  • CDC schedule changes (Jan 2026): 65% of HCPs aware; most say their recommendations won't change, but 2/3 expect fewer infants will receive RSV prevention overall (driven by parent refusal, not HCP advocacy reduction)
  • AAP > CDC in HCP trust: HCPs are following AAP/AAFP guidance over CDC/ACIP. Both societies support RSV immunization, which is a floor for the category.
  • FDA safety investigation (Dec 2025): Both Enflonsia and Beyfortus under review. No asymmetric finding yet, but creates background uncertainty.
  • Parent hesitancy: The biggest threat to both products is not each other -- it's a shrinking eligible pool driven by anti-vaccine sentiment.

Competitive Scorecard (Q1 2026)

Dimension Enflonsia Beyfortus Winner Trend
PP Reach 50% 80% Beyfortus Stable
NPP Reach 41% 95% Beyfortus Enflonsia gaining slightly
Share of Voice (PP) Growing Dominant Beyfortus Enflonsia gaining
Top Message ME 79% (ENF2) 72% (BEYF1) Enflonsia Stable
Brand Association (dosing) Strong, growing Moderate Enflonsia Improving
Brand Association (efficacy) Weak Strong Beyfortus Stable
Perception: Dosing/Admin Significantly higher -- Enflonsia Stable
Perception: Efficacy -- Significantly higher Beyfortus Stable
Perception: Safety -- Significantly higher Beyfortus Stable
High LTIS (stocking) ~33% ~40%+ Beyfortus Enflonsia improving
Rep Performance Trailing Leading Beyfortus Gap stable
Digital Paid Search Gaining (22% general RSV) Still dominant on own brand Mixed Enflonsia improving
Email Effectiveness Comparable quality More relevant content Beyfortus Stable

Integrated Data View: PET + Digital Tracker

Where PET and Digital Data Align

  • Dosing is king for Enflonsia -- Both PP and NPP data show ENF2 as the dominant message; digital tracker confirms Enflonsia's banner ads and emails consistently lead with dosing/FDA approval messaging
  • Beyfortus owns category-level visibility -- Higher reach across all channels (PP, NPP, paid search, banner ads, product websites, emails); digital tracker confirms broader browsing to Beyfortus sites
  • HCPs want practical information -- Paid search destinations show HCPs clicking through to product information and ordering pages; PET data shows cost/access is the #1 unmet conversation topic

Where They Diverge

  • Enflonsia is winning paid search on non-brand terms -- 22% share on general RSV keywords vs. 1% Beyfortus, suggesting Enflonsia's digital strategy is reaching category-seeking HCPs. But PET data shows this digital advantage hasn't translated into equivalent practice-level consideration yet.
  • Email parity vs. everything else -- Digital tracker shows comparable email reach and share, but PET data shows Beyfortus email content is rated more relevant. Quantity is matched; quality is not.

Recommendations for Merck (What We'd Advise if Asked)

Pre-Season 2026 (July-September)

  1. Surge PP reach -- Get Enflonsia rep reach to 65%+ before October. Every unreached practice is a lost season.
  2. Lead with ENF2 + ENF10 combination -- Data shows the dosing message pair (same dose + single IM injection) correlates with stronger rep evaluations and stocking intent.
  3. Arm reps for cost/access conversations -- HCPs are asking; reps aren't delivering. ENF14 (93% coverage) should be mandatory in every call.
  4. Deploy supply confidence messaging -- "Adequate supply for 2026-27" is a differentiator while Beyfortus shortage memory persists.

In-Season 2026-27

  1. Introduce RWE as soon as any data exists -- Even a small study or post-marketing safety update levels the conversation.
  2. Counter Beyfortus's second-season messaging -- Until Enflonsia has this indication, reframe: "For the vast majority of infants entering their first RSV season, Enflonsia provides equivalent protection with simpler administration."
  3. Expand email content beyond dosing/ordering -- Beyfortus's varied email campaigns (parent messaging, seasonal awareness, local RSV alerts) are rated more relevant.

Structural

  1. Fix rep performance -- Training investment on: (a) addressing HCP questions, (b) providing credible clinical support, (c) explaining cost/coverage
  2. File second-season indication ASAP -- SMART trial data supports it. Every month without this indication is a month Beyfortus has a moat.
  3. Track practice-level switching -- Individual HCP stocking data is more valuable than aggregate share-of-voice metrics in a binary-choice market.
